claim: Coherent stable ontology is the missing piece in agent design; agents need a world to live in because they can't recreate it themselves
actionable: Define explicit ontologies for your agent domains: entity types, relationships, constraints; treat it like a coherent codebase
source_channel: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
source_title: Ontologies, Evals & Deployed Intelligence: Building Real AI Agents (Basis CEO)

claim: Context is the gap-filler for agents lacking medium-term and long-term memory; humans use memory compression, agents need explicit context
actionable: Structure context explicitly for agents; use memory systems and context compression to avoid token bloat
source_channel: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
source_title: Ontologies, Evals & Deployed Intelligence: Building Real AI Agents (Basis CEO)

claim: Evals at scale are non-negotiable for production agents; without massive evals, agents aren't serious production systems
actionable: Build comprehensive evaluation suites before deploying agents; test across realistic scenarios, not just happy paths
source_channel: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
source_title: Ontologies, Evals & Deployed Intelligence: Building Real AI Agents (Basis CEO)

claim: Agents require system thinkers, not just coders; ontology building is coherent system design, not side-project hacking
actionable: Hire and develop systems thinking skills in agent teams; prioritize understanding domain data and architecture holistically
source_channel: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
source_title: Ontologies, Evals & Deployed Intelligence: Building Real AI Agents (Basis CEO)

claim: Deployed intelligence teams are needed to integrate agents into organizational workflows; automation alone isn't diffusion
actionable: Create dedicated roles for integrating agents into processes; don't assume agents deploy themselves into human workflows
source_channel: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
source_title: Ontologies, Evals & Deployed Intelligence: Building Real AI Agents (Basis CEO)

claim: Applied research in agents is solving open problems: memory systems, context compression, environment engineering, tool ontology
actionable: Invest in agent research for competitive advantage; areas like memory and context management are not yet solved
source_channel: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
source_title: Ontologies, Evals & Deployed Intelligence: Building Real AI Agents (Basis CEO)
